title: fix: consistent, locale-stable response count formatting in model table
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Supersedes #8876 (which targeted the pre-refactor path `src/client/components/pricing/calculations.ts`, now relocated to `frontend/src/components/models/`).

- Fixes #8875: `formatCount` rounded 975–999 to `"1000"` (`.toString()`) while all values ≥1000 went through `millify` → `"1K"`, so adjacent models showed `"1000"` vs `"1K"` in the same column.
- Drops the `millify` dependency — it was the sole consumer, and it abbreviates via `navigator`-locale `toLocaleString`, rendering `1050` as `"1,1K"` on non-en-US browsers. Every other formatter in the app builds compact strings manually and is locale-stable; replaced with a small inline K/M/B helper matching the existing chart style.
- All other displayed values unchanged (verified across the full range).

Verified: `tsc --noEmit` clean, biome clean, behavior table matches old output except the intended 975–999 → `"1K"`.

gist.headline: A thousand ways to say 1K
gist.blurb: Fixed a quirky bug where 999 responses showed up as '1000' right next to '1K'. We also dropped a dependency to make sure numbers look right no matter where you live.
gist.summary: Replaced the millify dependency with a custom K/M/B formatter to fix inconsistent rounding (975-999 showing as '1000' instead of '1K') and locale-specific comma issues in the model table.
gist.impact: Response counts in the model table now consistently use 'K' for thousands and format correctly regardless of your browser's locale settings.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing numbers or blocks with '1K' on them, maybe sweeping away a '1000' block. A cute robot looking at a clean table of numbers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot organizing glowing blocks with '1K' written on them, while a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head happily points at a clean data table. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
